This text does not seem to relate to evolution
/* Evolution as a collection of theories not fact */ an advanced theory of narrow scope
/* Evolution as a collection of theories not fact */  Citing SJ Gould: Calling a law a [[scientific fact|fact]] is [[ambiguous]], an [[overstatement]], or an [[equivocation]].

/* Evolution as a collection of theories not fact */  Newton's [[law of universal gravitation]] is an example of an advanced theory that describes the [[empirical evidence|observable facts]] and predicts future facts. That is does so with a notable degree of reliability has led the scientific community to unanimously describe it as a natural law.
/* Evolution as a collection of theories not fact */

"certainty" is a noun, "confirmed" is a verb; it is bad English to compare them. turning "certainty" to "known with certainty" fixes it. Also, quotations should be sourced.
/* top */  The second sentence is ungrammatical. I'm fixing that aspect but if the intended meaning is something else, edit it further.
